---
title: "Epstein Files — Network Analysis"
---

This project analyzes the publicly released Epstein Files — legal documents, emails, and
court records published by the U.S. Department of Justice and the U.S. House Oversight
Committee. Using Named Entity Recognition (NER) and network analysis, it maps the
documented relationships between individuals and institutions named in the corpus,
and identifies actors who functioned as **structural bridges** between otherwise
disconnected social clusters.

![The visualization product development process](pics/vizproductprocess.png){#fig-vizprocess}

The work follows the visualization product development process shown in @fig-vizprocess,
based on the concepts outlined in @DoemerKempf2022_1000150238.

---

## Research Question

> *Which network structure underlies the documented connections of Jeffrey Epstein, and which
> persons or institutions functioned as structural bridges between otherwise separate domains?*

The analysis is grounded in Burt's concept of **structural holes** — network positions
where a single actor serves as the primary bridge between clusters that would otherwise
have no direct connection. Actors occupying structural holes have unique informational and
relational leverage; identifying them in a document corpus is a way to operationalise
influence from text alone.

---

## Approach

The pipeline proceeds in four stages:

1. **Sampling** — a stratified sample of 1 000 PDF documents per sub-collection is
   streamed from HuggingFace (`Nikity/Epstein-Files`), preserving coverage across all
   document sets. See [Data Report](data_report.qmd).

2. **Entity Extraction** — spaCy NER identifies persons (`PERSON`) and organisations
   (`ORG`) in each document. Entities are normalised and filtered to those appearing in
   at least 3 documents.

3. **Network Construction** — two entities share an edge if they co-occur in the same
   document. Edge weight equals the number of documents they share. The resulting
   co-occurrence graph is the analytical substrate for all metrics.

4. **Structural Analysis** — betweenness centrality identifies bridge actors (structural
   holes). Louvain community detection surfaces domain clusters within the network.
   See [Visualization Design](viz_design_report.qmd).

---

## Project Sections

| Section | Content |
|---|---|
| [Project Charta](project_charta.qmd) | Research question, objectives, stakeholders, project plan |
| [Data Report](data_report.qmd) | Dataset description, EDA results, processed data |
| [Visualization Design](viz_design_report.qmd) | Design decisions, interaction concept, tech stack |
| [Evaluation](evaluation.qmd) | Assessment against success criteria, limitations |
| [Meeting Notes](meeting_notes.qmd) | Project log |

---

## Team

Gruppe 7 — ZHAW, Visualization & Data Science module, Spring 2026.

| Name | Contact |
|---|---|
| Sendogan Kulakci | kulaksen@students.zhaw.ch |
| Lewis Birrer | birrelew@students.zhaw.ch |
